Name: Aerial Cirque Over Denver
Hours: Morning, 9am to noon. Afternoon, 12 to 3pm. Full day, 9am to 3pm
Weeks Run: Thanksgiving week and Holiday break (2 weeks)
Location: Central Park (4605 Quebec St. Denver, CO. 80216)
Notes: Camps are for ages 6 – teen. This is a beginner/intermediate aerial camp perfect for those brand new to aerial or with less than a year of aerial experience. In the week long camp your kiddo will learn technique and skills on 4 apparatuses: silks, trapeze, hoop, sling. All our instructors are certified and have experience teaching youth aerialists - our focus is on safety and fun!
Ages/Grades: Learn aerial silks, trapeze, hoop, and hammock. PLEASE REVIEW: our covid policies at the top of the page! Your registration confirms your agreement. Please note there are no refunds for any reason, including changing covid policies; we follow CDC and city guidelines.

Description: Drop your kids at the shop for a day of good old-fashioned making and doing. Each day off is a different art, craft, or DIY theme that will keep your child happy and engaged all day long. We provide loads of creative fun, outdoor playtime, and a safe, supportive atmosphere for exploration. Register by the day. 10% sibling discount.
Notes:
NEW age clarification: Your kiddo must be at least 5 years old and in kindergarten to attend school break camps at C&a.  
We frequently use real tools, sharp scissors, and low-temp hot glue guns. Safety precautions, talks, and checks are in full effect. Doing dangerous things safely is our jam.

Description: Looking for something amazing for your kids to do during school breaks? At the Museum, your child will experience fun and wonder, see planetarium shows, and explore the marvels of our temporary exhibitions. Name: Grasshopper
Hours: Select week(s) and morning (9A-12P) or afternoon (1P-4P) camp
Cost Per Week: Every experience is 2 hours in length and happens at your home. For up to 2 kids, the cost is $120 total. When 3 or more children participate in an experience, the cost is reduced to $50 per child. Up to 6 kids, depending on the experience.
Description:
A new Kind of camp
A week of engaging, in-person experiences your kids will love led by teachers who come to you. Choose mornings or afternoons.

Name: JCC Winter Break
Hours:
Sunrise Care: 7:30 am – 8:30 am
School’s Out! Program: 8:30 am – 3:30 pm
Sunset Care: 3:30 – 4:30 pm
Weeks Run:
December 23 – Jan 6
*No camp on December 25 & January 1
Cost Per Week: $100/day, sunrise and sunset care available for additional fee
Discounts Offered: 20% off of the enrollment cost for additional siblings.
Lunch: Nut-free facility, they provide morning and afternoon snack but not lunch
Description:
December 23-27 Join us for a magical winter break filled with joy and celebration as we celebrate the Festival of Lights! Campers will enjoy winter-themed arts and crafts, science experiments, dreidel games, and swimming in our indoor pool. We’ll also focus on sharing acts of kindness, helping everyone feel connected to each other, and strengthening our camp community. Also, your camper can show their holiday spirit on Cozy Sweater Day—they can wear their coziest or silliest sweater on December 24!

This is a great chance for all campers to celebrate the season of miracles and create lasting memories! Whether you’re celebrating Hanukkah or just looking for fun with friends, we can’t wait to welcome you to School’s Out!
Notes:
December 30 – January 3
Welcome to Camp Shai’s Arctic Adventure, where winter excitement is in the air! This winter break, your child can dive into hands-on science experiments exploring the wonders of the Arctic, unleash their creativity with fun Arctic-themed crafts, and enjoy splashing around in our (heated) indoor pool. With plenty of engaging games and activities, campers will have a blast making new friends and learning all about life in the world’s coldest environment!

Plus, we’ll be welcoming the new year in the coziest way possible with a pajama party on December 31!
Ages/Grades: At least 5, and have completed one year of schooling

Name: JuneBugs Outdoor Winter Break Camp
Description: JuneBugs is an all outdoor nature camp for kids! We explore the local ecology of the park through mindfulness, crafts, games, and most importantly, PLAY! Give your child the gift of time spend outside this winter break with JuneBugs!
Notes: JuneBugs is a small forest-school-inspired summer camp run out of Washington Park in Denver, Colorado.  This is a half-day program catering to children aged 3-7; where all of our activities will be completely outdoors! We will explore our local ecology, do art projects, team-building exercises, STEAM activities, and story-telling right out of our favorite local urban park.

Name: The Spot Climbing Gym
Description: Our Climbing Camps have everything your child needs to stay active and engaged. Campers will build climbing skills, confidence and friendships while participating in arts and crafts projects, team building activities and everything that makes camps so memorable. Campers will walk away with a larger appreciation for the outdoor community and skills that will last a lifetime.

Name: SPACE - 'Little Wellness Retreat'
Weeks Run: First two week days in January
Location: 3810 Pierce St, Unit B, Wheat Ridge CO 80033
Lunch:
Morning Session: Healthy snacks included, BYO Lunch
Afternoon Session, Healthy snacks included
Interesting Camp Sessions: Kids can paint on the walls! LWR's are built around mindfulness through creative, movement and communal activities
Description: An intentionally designed, half-day wellness experience for kids 5+. Little Wellness Retreats instills mindfulness through communal and creative exercises that build confidence and connection to self in a comforting and supportive space.
Ages/Grades:
Ages 5+
We intentionally keep this mixed-age to reflect true community. Younger and older children can learn from each other, build social-emotional skills, and develop confidence as they move through the day. 11-year-olds will step into leadership and 5-year-olds shine with a little support from a new friend.
